title: Model Context Protocol
oneLiner: An open protocol for connecting AI apps to tools and context.
summary: Model Context Protocol is an open protocol for connecting AI applications to external tools, data sources, and structured context providers.
whyItMatters: As agents connect to more tools and data, integration needs a shared protocol. MCP gives builders a common shape for servers, clients, and tool access.
notFor: Single-purpose apps with no external context
seoTitle: Model Context Protocol: Open protocol for AI tools
seoDescription: A concise editorial profile of Model Context Protocol, including what it does, who it is best for, repo, docs, and related open-source AI resources.
shareDescription: An open protocol for connecting AI apps to tools and context.
featuredReason: A core protocol layer for agent-ready tool ecosystems.

coreStrengths.title: Shared tool connection protocol
coreStrengths.description: MCP gives AI apps, clients, and servers a common protocol for exposing tools and context.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: A shared protocol lowers the cost of connecting agents to useful systems without one-off integration patterns every time.

coreStrengths.title: Context provider architecture
coreStrengths.description: The protocol is designed around external systems providing structured context, tools, and data to AI applications.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: This helps turn agents from isolated chat windows into systems that can work with real resources.

coreStrengths.title: Ecosystem interoperability
coreStrengths.description: MCP is useful because clients and servers can meet around a predictable interface instead of bespoke plugin APIs.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: Interoperability is one of the foundations for a healthier open agent ecosystem.

useCaseNotes.title: Tool servers for agents
useCaseNotes.description: Use MCP when exposing a tool, data source, or workflow endpoint to compatible AI clients.

useCaseNotes.title: Agent-ready integrations
useCaseNotes.description: A fit for teams that want integrations to work across multiple clients rather than a single custom app.

useCaseNotes.title: Structured context access
useCaseNotes.description: Useful when an AI app needs controlled access to files, services, or domain-specific systems.

compareNotes.title: Choose it when interoperability matters
compareNotes.summary: A custom API can be faster for one product, but MCP is more relevant when the same integration should serve multiple agent clients.

compareNotes.title: Protocol adoption is the key risk
compareNotes.summary: The value of MCP grows with compatible clients and servers, so evaluate ecosystem fit as much as the specification itself.

thumbnailBrief.visualMotif: connectors, ports, and structured protocol modules
